rules: Use master-gate stage as the source of truth; do not skip profile_setup.
Profile setup and first-batch publishing may be complete; KPI, weekly review, lead, and offer decisions remain evidence-gated.
Every external action needs visible account identity, safe copy, public URL verification, and proof note.
Placeholder post URLs must be rejected until real public post URLs exist; after writeback, KPI source proof becomes the blocker.
Exception runbook stop conditions override all quickstarts.

steps.release: Three profile setup copy blocks and profile proof templates are ready.
steps.stop: Stop if the visible platform account is not LoveTypes or the profile copy adds paid, diagnosis, therapy, or guarantee claims.

steps.release: All profile links are set/live with traceable proof notes.
steps.stop: Do not write back profile status from memory, draft screens, private previews, or missing screenshots.
Do not publish first-batch posts while profile setup remains incomplete.

steps.release: First batch posts have public URLs and checked minimum KPI rows.
steps.stop: Do not open weekly review from placeholder URLs, private posts, or unchecked zero KPI values.

steps.release: Stop/hold/escalate conditions are visible before every external platform action.
steps.stop: Stop immediately on wrong account, missing permission, URL rewrite, unsafe copy, or emergency/support request.

profileRows.action: Set the platform profile link, then verify the structured proof text before writeback.
profileRows.success_signal: profile proof import validates and platform tracker row can be set/live with real proof.
profileRows.stop_condition: A real platform screenshot/click proof must exist before running the write command.

stopRows.trigger: Visible account, channel, public profile, or edit permission does not match the intended LoveTypes platform identity.
stopRows.stop: Do not set profile link, do not publish, and do not write back profile status.
stopRows.recovery: Switch to the correct account, rerun platform account identity checklist, then capture a traceable proof note.
stopRows.operatorAction: If trigger appears, follow stop condition first, then recovery condition.

stopRows.trigger: Profile link is not the planned /start/ UTM URL, is missing, or cannot be clicked from the public profile.
stopRows.stop: Do not mark profile set/live and do not publish first-batch posts.
stopRows.recovery: Fix the profile link, verify it opens lovetypes.tw/start/ with UTM preserved, then run profile text import check/add.
stopRows.operatorAction: If trigger appears, follow stop condition first, then recovery condition.

stopRows.trigger: A first-batch post is about to publish while ready_to_publish is false.
stopRows.stop: Cancel or leave draft/scheduled; do not publish manually around the gate.
stopRows.recovery: Complete all active profile rows, refresh launch readiness, then use the first-batch publish action sheet.
stopRows.operatorAction: If trigger appears, follow stop condition first, then recovery condition.

stopRows.trigger: Post URL domain does not match the active platform row, or URL points to the wrong post.
stopRows.stop: Do not write back; do not reuse the URL in weekly review.
stopRows.recovery: Find the correct public post URL for the platform row and rerun post text import check.
stopRows.operatorAction: If trigger appears, follow stop condition first, then recovery condition.

stopRows.trigger: Caption/profile first action emphasizes Luna, affiliate books, paid products, or purchase before the quiz.
stopRows.stop: Do not publish, or edit immediately before public verification/writeback.
stopRows.recovery: Restore the single primary CTA: complete the 15-question quiz and find the emotional guardian.
stopRows.operatorAction: If trigger appears, follow stop condition first, then recovery condition.

stopRows.trigger: Offer order, paid CTA, Luna emphasis, affiliate emphasis, or winning guardian is changed while empty-data mode is true.
stopRows.stop: Revert the commerce/content-priority change and keep collect_signal focus.
stopRows.recovery: Wait until first-batch evidence, KPI rows, weekly review and decision gates are ready.
stopRows.operatorAction: If trigger appears, follow stop condition first, then recovery condition.
